title: pneumonanthe macrophylla
intro: Pneumonanthe macrophylla, also known as lungwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. Traditionally, the leaves of the plant have been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its expectorant properties, helping to ease congestion and coughs by stimulating mucus production. The medicinal properties of Pneumonanthe macrophylla are attributed to its saponins, flavonoids, and other compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Pneumonanthe macrophylla can be consumed as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide relief from respiratory issues and other health concerns.
